highlights.text: My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
They occur when - and only when - price puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, which is the footprint of institutional accumulation.

Instead of just buying this, a safer bet is to wait for the Failed Breakdown of today’s low which is just a little above at 5607.” We saw exactly this yesterday, we sold down to 5597-5600, put in a Failed Breakdown of Tuesdays lows at 5607-05 in the process, then rallied up the range to ~5667 into the close.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into a high quality Failed Breakdown we had yesterday (discussed above) and this was a textbook example of my core setup type.

post_title: Breakout To New Highs For SPX. Is It A Trap? May 9th Plan
